title: Multi-Modal Hallucination Control by Visual Information Grounding
key_contributions: Empirically demonstrates that visual prompt dependency decreases as more tokens are generated
Introduces the concept of 'conditioning dilution' or 'fading memory effect'
Proposes M3ID as an intervention to maximize visual prompt dependency
Proposes a training objective using Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) for users with access to model weights
problems_addressed: Generating plausible-sounding answers without factual basis
Models over-relying on unconditioned language priors

method_details.approach: Intervenes in the generative distribution by finding tokens that 'surprise' the unconditioned VLM
method_details.mechanism: Amplifies conditioned directions that are not already predicted by the unconditioned model
method_details.parameter: Uses a progressively smaller gamma_t parameter as new tokens are generated
method_details.evaluation: Compares likelihood of generating output with and without visual information
method_details.optimization: Maximizes mutual information between text output tokens and the visual prompt
method_details.implementation: Rescales image-conditioned component against unconditioned distribution

advantages: Applicable to any off-the-shelf model without additional training or access to model weights
Offers low computational overhead compared to standard decoding algorithms
Preserves linguistic fluency of the original model
